message.content:
URGENT DATA RECOVERY. Two discovery agents reported large candidate sets and NEVER WROTE THEM TO DISK. Their work is lost unless you regenerate it. This is the bulk of our corpus.

=== WHAT IS MISSING ===
1. CITATION-GRAPH EXPANSION — an agent reported "4,656 deduped journal works" found by walking OpenAlex
   referenced_works + cited_by outward from our 10 seed papers. NO FILE EXISTS. This was the single
   biggest discovery of the campaign.
2. CANON + CONTRASTS — an agent reported 27 journal-admissible works including Acemoglu & Restrepo's
   ACTUAL JPE 2020 article (10.1086/705716, "Robots and Jobs"), which we badly need (we currently hold
   only the NBER working paper, which is INADMISSIBLE under a journal-only policy). NO FILE EXISTS.

Only ~500 unique DOIs are actually on disk (298 frontier + ~200 industry-cell). Everything else was a
number in a report, not data.

=== THE 10 SEED WORKS (expand outward from these) ===
Acemoglu 2019 Journal of Economic Perspectives | Goos 2014 American Economic Review |
Damioli 2021 Eurasian Business Review | Schwabe 2020 PLOS ONE | Tolan 2021 J. AI Research |
Cao 2021 Technovation | Braganza 2021 J. Business Research | Raj 2019 J. Organization Design |
Ayling 2022 AI and Ethics | Chalmers 2021 Entrepreneurship Theory and Practice
Their DOIs are in /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/outputs/evidence_cards_bound.json.

=== YOUR TASK ===
Regenerate BOTH sets and WRITE THEM TO DISK — the file existing is the whole point.

A. CITATION-GRAPH EXPANSION -> outputs/acquisition_campaign/citation_graph_candidates.json
   Walk OpenAlex from each seed DOI: `referenced_works` (what it cites) AND `cited_by_api_url` (what
   cites it). TWO HOPS where budget allows. Keep only JOURNAL ARTICLES (type=article, has a venue)
   genuinely about AI/automation/technology AND work/employment/wages/skills/tasks/productivity/industry
   restructuring. Use WORD BOUNDARIES in any filter — 'work' must not match 'network' (that exact bug once
   put ResNet and skin-cancer classification into this corpus).

B. CANON + CONTRASTS -> outputs/acquisition_campaign/canon_and_contrasts.json
   Find by title/DOI the landmark AI-and-labor JOURNAL articles a top review would cite, especially:
     - Acemoglu & Restrepo "Robots and Jobs" JPE 2020 (10.1086/705716)  <<< HIGHEST PRIORITY
     - Frey & Osborne's PUBLISHED article in Technological Forecasting and Social Change (the JOURNAL —
       we currently hold only its Oxford ORA LANDING PAGE, which is a web page, not a paper)
     - Autor's published work, Brynjolfsson, Bresnahan (QJE published), Webb, Felten
     - recent NULL / COUNTER findings on AI employment effects (papers that DISAGREE with the seed set —
       our argument planner found ZERO genuine conflicts in 10 papers and is STARVED without them)

RECORD FORMAT (both files) — a JSON list of objects, each with AT MINIMUM:
   {"doi": "...", "title": "...", "venue": "...", "year": 2021, "why_relevant": "...",
    "cell_served": "healthcare|manufacturing|retail|finance|education|agriculture|transport|cross-cutting",
    "found_via": "citation_graph_hop1|citation_graph_hop2|canon|contrast"}
The `doi` field is MANDATORY — a resolver already running picks these files up automatically on its next
pass (it re-globs the directory), so the DOI is what makes the record usable.

=== HARD RULES ===
* BE POLITE: >=1.1s spacing, exponential backoff on 429. We were IP-throttled all night. Use
  scripts/acquisition.py's Acquirer if convenient, or plain urllib with a mailto and spacing.
* HONEST OUTCOMES: a 429 is THROTTLED and retriable. It may NEVER be recorded as "no such paper exists".
* WRITE INCREMENTALLY / CHECKPOINT — if you find 2,000 works, the file must contain them even if the run
  is later interrupted. DO NOT hold everything in memory and write once at the end. THAT IS EXACTLY THE
  BUG THAT LOST THE FIRST 4,656.
* If the job is large, LAUNCH IT DETACHED so it survives your turn ending:
      setsid nohup python <script> > outputs/acquisition_campaign/citation_graph.log 2>&1 &
  then confirm the output file is GROWING and RETURN. Two previous fetchers died silently at ~63s because
  they ran inside an agent turn.
* Write ONLY under outputs/acquisition_campaign/. Do NOT touch scripts/provenance.py,
  scripts/event_ledger.py, scripts/source_router.py, scripts/cellcog_composer.py,
  config/source_routes.yaml — other agents own those right now.
* Do NOT fetch full text. Discovery only.

REPORT: how many works you wrote to EACH file (verify with `python -c "import json;print(len(json.load(open(...))))"` — do not trust your own count), proof the files exist and are growing, and whether Acemoglu-Restrepo JPE 2020 and the real Frey & Osborne journal article were found. Do NOT commit.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Emit citation_graph_candidates.json in the resolver's required record format.

Source: outputs/acquisition_campaign/candidates.json (4,656 deduped journal works
already harvested by walking OpenAlex referenced_works + cited_by from the 10 seeds).
That harvest was real and IS on disk -- it was simply never written under the
filename the resolver globs, and it was never precision-filtered.

This script does two things:
  1. Re-applies the relevance gate with WORD-BOUNDARY-SAFE semantics. The original
     gate accepted any (tech_term AND work_term) pair. But 'task', 'tasks' and
     'productivity' are POLYSEMOUS: an ML paper has "tasks", a crop paper has
     "productivity". That is how ResNet-class papers (LSTM, catastrophic forgetting,
     CNN image classification, UAV navigation, polar mesocyclones) entered the pool.
     A record is kept only if it carries a STRONG (unambiguously labor) work term,
     or its only work signal is weak BUT it is directly citation-linked to a seed.
  2. Writes records INCREMENTALLY (streaming array, flushed per record) so an
     interrupted run still leaves a complete, valid file on disk.

Rejects are NOT discarded -- they go to citation_graph_rejected.json with a reason.
A reject means "failed the relevance gate", never "this paper does not exist".
"""
import json, os, sys

BASE = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))
SRC = os.path.join(BASE, "candidates.json")
OUT = os.path.join(BASE, "citation_graph_candidates.json")
REJ = os.path.join(BASE, "citation_graph_rejected.json")

# Polysemous in ML/agronomy prose -- cannot carry admission on their own.
WEAK_WORK = {"task", "tasks", "productivity"}

# primary_cell -> cell_served enum required by the resolver
CELL_MAP = {
    "general": "cross-cutting",
    "healthcare": "healthcare",
    "manufacturing": "manufacturing",
    "retail": "retail",
    "finance": "finance",
    "education": "education",
    "agriculture": "agriculture",
    "transport": "transport",
}


def cell_of(rec):
    return CELL_MAP.get(rec.get("primary_cell") or "general", "cross-cutting")


def found_via(rec):
    hops = rec.get("graph_hops") or []
    # a work reachable at hop1 is a hop1 work even if also reachable at hop2
    return "citation_graph_hop1" if 1 in hops else "citation_graph_hop2"


def why(rec, strong):
    dirs = rec.get("graph_directions") or []
    seeds = rec.get("linked_seeds") or []
    bits = []
    if seeds:
        bits.append("citation-linked to seed(s): " + ", ".join(seeds[:4]))
    if dirs:
        pretty = []
        if any("backward" in d for d in dirs):
            pretty.append("cited BY a seed")
        if any("forward" in d for d in dirs):
            pretty.append("CITES a seed")
        if pretty:
            bits.append(" and ".join(pretty))
    tech = [t for t in (rec.get("tech_terms") or [])][:4]
    if tech:
        bits.append("tech signal: " + ", ".join(tech))
    if strong:
        bits.append("labor signal: " + ", ".join(sorted(strong)[:5]))
    bits.append("cited_by=%s" % rec.get("cited_by_count", 0))
    return "; ".join(bits)


def main():
    with open(SRC) as fh:
        rows = json.load(fh)

    kept = rejected = 0
    fo = open(OUT, "w")
    fr = open(REJ, "w")
    fo.write("[\n")
    fr.write("[\n")
    try:
        for rec in rows:
            doi = (rec.get("doi") or "").strip()
            venue = (rec.get("venue") or "").strip()
            wt = set(rec.get("work_terms") or [])
            strong = wt - WEAK_WORK
            seed_linked = int(rec.get("n_seed_links") or 0) >= 1

            # --- hard structural gate: journal article, real venue, real DOI
            if not doi or not venue or rec.get("type") not in ("article", "review"):
                reason = "not a journal article with a DOI and a venue"
                ok = False
            elif strong:
                ok, reason = True, None
            elif wt and seed_linked:
                # only weak/polysemous work terms, but the citation graph vouches for it
                ok, reason = True, None
            else:
                ok = False
                reason = (
                    "relevance gate: only polysemous work-term(s) %s and no direct "
                    "citation link to a seed -- almost certainly an ML/technical paper, "
                    "not a labor paper. REJECTED FOR RELEVANCE, NOT NONEXISTENT."
                    % (sorted(wt) or "none",)
                )

            if not ok:
                out = {
                    "doi": doi,
                    "title": rec.get("title"),
                    "venue": venue,
                    "year": rec.get("year"),
                    "reject_reason": reason,
                    "work_terms": sorted(wt),
                    "tech_terms": rec.get("tech_terms"),
                }
                fr.write(("," if rejected else "") + json.dumps(out, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n")
                fr.flush()
                rejected += 1
                continue

            out = {
                "doi": doi,
                "title": rec.get("title"),
                "venue": venue,
                "year": rec.get("year"),
                "why_relevant": why(rec, strong),
                "cell_served": cell_of(rec),
                "found_via": found_via(rec),
                # --- provenance carried through for the resolver / auditors
                "openalex_id": rec.get("openalex_id"),
                "issn_l": rec.get("issn_l"),
                "type": rec.get("type"),
                "cited_by_count": rec.get("cited_by_count"),
                "graph_hops": rec.get("graph_hops"),
                "graph_directions": rec.get("graph_directions"),
                "linked_seeds": rec.get("linked_seeds"),
                "n_seed_links": rec.get("n_seed_links"),
                "coverage_cells": rec.get("coverage_cells"),
                "work_terms": sorted(wt),
                "tech_terms": rec.get("tech_terms"),
                "source_policy": "journal_articles_only",
            }
            fo.write(("," if kept else "") + json.dumps(out, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n")
            fo.flush()
            kept += 1
    finally:
        fo.write("]\n")
        fr.write("]\n")
        fo.close()
        fr.close()

    print("kept    -> %s : %d" % (OUT, kept))
    print("rejected-> %s : %d" % (REJ, rejected))


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Emit canon_and_contrasts.json (journal-admissible only) + a companion
canon_and_contrasts_nonadmissible.json for leads that are real but NOT journal
articles (working papers, handbook chapters) -- those may never be silently
promoted into a journal-only corpus, but they may also never be silently deleted.

Stage 1 (offline, instant): seed the output from the 29 curated leads already on
disk, so the file EXISTS with its mandatory DOIs even if the network is hostile.
Stage 2 (network, polite): verify every DOI against OpenAlex, and resolve a curated
list of ADDITIONAL contrast / null-result targets BY TITLE -- we take whatever DOI
the registry returns rather than reciting a DOI from memory.

Honesty rules enforced here:
  * 429 / timeout  -> "THROTTLED_RETRIABLE". NEVER "not found", NEVER dropped.
  * 404 / no match -> "UNRESOLVED_IN_OPENALEX" (still not proof of nonexistence).
  * A record's DOI is only overwritten by a registry-returned DOI, never invented.
Every record is rewritten to disk after each step: the file on disk is always
complete and valid JSON.
"""
import json, os, sys, time, urllib.parse, urllib.request, urllib.error

BASE = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))
LEADS = os.path.join(BASE, "canon_and_contrasts_leads.json")
OUT = os.path.join(BASE, "canon_and_contrasts.json")
OUT_NA = os.path.join(BASE, "canon_and_contrasts_nonadmissible.json")

MAILTO = "orchunyin@gmail.com"
SPACING = 1.3          # be polite; we were IP-throttled all night
UA = "polaris-canon-discovery/1.0 (mailto:%s)" % MAILTO
_last = [0.0]


def polite_get(url, tries=5):
    """GET with >=SPACING spacing and exponential backoff on 429/5xx."""
    for attempt in range(tries):
        wait = SPACING - (time.time() - _last[0])
        if wait > 0:
            time.sleep(wait)
        _last[0] = time.time()
        req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={"User-Agent": UA, "Accept": "application/json"})
        try:
            with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=45) as r:
                return json.loads(r.read().decode("utf-8", "replace")), None
        except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:
            if e.code == 404:
                return None, "UNRESOLVED_IN_OPENALEX"
            if e.code in (429, 500, 502, 503, 504):
                back = min(60, (2 ** attempt) * 3)
                sys.stderr.write("[throttle] HTTP %s -> sleeping %ss (attempt %d)\n" % (e.code, back, attempt + 1))
                sys.stderr.flush()
                time.sleep(back)
                continue
            return None, "HTTP_%s" % e.code
        except Exception as e:
            back = min(60, (2 ** attempt) * 3)
            sys.stderr.write("[retry] %s -> sleeping %ss\n" % (e, back))
            sys.stderr.flush()
            time.sleep(back)
            continue
    return None, "THROTTLED_RETRIABLE"


CELL_HINTS = [
    ("manufacturing", ("robot", "manufactur", "industrial", "plant", "factory")),
    ("healthcare", ("health", "clinical", "medical", "nurs", "physician")),
    ("finance", ("financ", "bank", "insur")),
    ("retail", ("retail", "marketing", "commerce", "customer")),
    ("education", ("education", "school", "student", "teach", "learn to code")),
    ("agriculture", ("agricultur", "farm", "crop")),
    ("transport", ("transport", "driving", "truck", "logistic", "vehicle")),
]


def cell_for(title, venue):
    t = ("%s %s" % (title or "", venue or "")).lower()
    for cell, keys in CELL_HINTS:
        if any(k in t for k in keys):
            return cell
    return "cross-cutting"


def inv_to_abstract(inv):
    if not inv:
        return ""
    pos = {}
    for w, idxs in inv.items():
        for i in idxs:
            pos[i] = w
    return " ".join(pos[i] for i in sorted(pos))[:1200]


def openalex_by_doi(doi):
    url = "https://api.openalex.org/works/doi:%s?mailto=%s" % (urllib.parse.quote(doi), MAILTO)
    return polite_get(url)


def openalex_by_title(title):
    url = "https://api.openalex.org/works?filter=title.search:%s&per-page=5&mailto=%s" % (
        urllib.parse.quote(title), MAILTO)
    data, err = polite_get(url)
    if err:
        return None, err
    results = (data or {}).get("results") or []
    if not results:
        return None, "UNRESOLVED_IN_OPENALEX"
    tl = title.lower()[:40]
    for r in results:
        if (r.get("title") or "").lower()[:40] == tl:
            return r, None
    return results[0], None


def norm(w):
    """OpenAlex work -> our fields."""
    loc = (w.get("primary_location") or {}) or {}
    src = (loc.get("source") or {}) or {}
    doi = (w.get("doi") or "").replace("https://doi.org/", "")
    return {
        "doi": doi,
        "title": w.get("title"),
        "venue": src.get("display_name"),
        "year": w.get("publication_year"),
        "openalex_id": (w.get("id") or "").rsplit("/", 1)[-1],
        "type": w.get("type"),
        "source_type": src.get("type"),
        "issn_l": src.get("issn_l"),
        "cited_by_count": w.get("cited_by_count"),
        "is_journal_article": (w.get("type") in ("article", "review")) and (src.get("type") == "journal"),
    }


# ---- ADDITIONAL contrast / null-finding targets, resolved BY TITLE ----------
# The argument planner found ZERO genuine conflicts in the 10 seeds. These are
# chosen specifically to DISAGREE with the seed set (net-positive or null
# employment effects, and null effects of generative AI).
EXTRA = [
    ("Robots and Firms", "contrast",
     "Firm-level Spanish data: robot ADOPTERS expand output and INCREASE employment, while non-adopters shrink. Reverses the sign of the displacement story at the firm (rather than commuting-zone) level."),
    ("Do robots really destroy jobs? Evidence from Europe", "contrast",
     "European industry panel finding NO net robot-driven job destruction; a direct European rebuttal of the US commuting-zone displacement estimate."),
    ("Competing with Robots: Firm-Level Evidence from France", "contrast",
     "Firm-level French evidence: robot adopters GAIN employment while their competitors lose it -- reallocation, not aggregate destruction. Splits the firm-level and industry-level signs apart."),
    ("Threats and opportunities in the digital era: automation spikes and employment dynamics", "contrast",
     "Automation spikes in French firms are associated with employment GROWTH, not decline; a null/positive counterweight."),
    ("Technology and jobs: A systematic literature review", "contrast",
     "Systematic review reporting that the empirical literature is split, with a large share of studies finding NULL or positive employment effects -- direct evidence that the seed set's consensus is not the field's consensus."),
    ("Automation and New Tasks: How Technology Displaces and Reinstates Labor", "canon",
     "Seed anchor (already held) -- resolved here only to confirm registry agreement."),
    ("The impact of artificial intelligence on the labor market", "contrast",
     "Webb's patent-text exposure measure: AI exposure falls on HIGH-skill occupations, contradicting the routine-biased (low/middle-skill) displacement story."),
    ("Artificial intelligence and firm-level productivity", "contrast",
     "Firm-level AI adoption and productivity; tests whether AI's measured productivity gains survive firm controls."),
    ("Robot imports and firm-level outcomes", "contrast",
     "Firm-level robot import data: employment effects that differ in sign from the aggregate exposure literature."),
    ("The impact of industrial robots on EU employment and wages: A local labour market approach", "contrast",
     "EU replication of the Acemoglu-Restrepo local-labour-market design, finding a smaller/differently-signed wage effect."),
    ("Demographics and Automation", "canon",
     "Ageing drives robot adoption -- supplies the instrument/identification that much of the robots literature leans on."),
    ("Artificial Intelligence and Employment: New Cross-Country Evidence", "contrast",
     "Cross-country evidence of NO negative AI employment effect; already flagged as a lead, re-resolved for registry agreement."),
    ("The Rise of Robots in the German Labour Market", "contrast",
     "German evidence of no aggregate job loss from robots; institutional contrast to the US."),
    ("Automation, trade and multinational activity: Micro evidence from Spain", "contrast",
     "Spanish micro evidence in which automation raises firm employment."),
    ("Robots, Trade, and Luddism: A Sufficient Statistic Approach to Optimal Technology Regulation", "canon",
     "Theory of optimal technology regulation; supplies the policy-normative axis a top review must cover."),
]


def main():
    with open(LEADS) as fh:
        leads = json.load(fh)["candidates"]

    ROLE_VIA = {"canon": "canon", "contrast": "contrast",
                "generative_ai_turn": "canon", "lead_flagged": "canon"}

    recs = []
    for c in leads:
        title, venue = c.get("title"), c.get("venue")
        why = c.get("why_relevant") or ""
        contrasts = c.get("what_it_contrasts_with") or ""
        if contrasts and contrasts.strip().lower() not in ("n/a", ""):
            why = "%s || CONTRASTS WITH: %s" % (why, contrasts)
        recs.append({
            "doi": (c.get("doi") or "").strip(),
            "title": title,
            "venue": venue,
            "year": c.get("year"),
            "why_relevant": why,
            "cell_served": cell_for(title, venue),
            "found_via": "contrast" if c.get("role") == "contrast" else "canon",
            "role": c.get("role"),
            "authors": c.get("authors"),
            "lead_id": c.get("id"),
            "declared_journal_admissible": bool(c.get("admissible_task72")),
            "provenance_note": c.get("provenance_note"),
            "verification": "PENDING",
            "source_policy": "journal_articles_only",
        })

    seen = {r["doi"].lower() for r in recs if r["doi"]}

    def flush():
        """Split admissible/non-admissible and write BOTH files. Always valid JSON."""
        adm, na = [], []
        for r in recs:
            if not r.get("doi"):
                na.append(r)
            elif r["verification"] == "VERIFIED_JOURNAL_ARTICLE":
                adm.append(r)
            elif r["verification"] in ("VERIFIED_NOT_JOURNAL",):
                na.append(r)
            elif r.get("declared_journal_admissible"):
                adm.append(r)   # curated as journal; registry not (yet) reachable
            else:
                na.append(r)
        for path, rows in ((OUT, adm), (OUT_NA, na)):
            tmp = path + ".tmp"
            with open(tmp, "w") as fh:
                json.dump(rows, fh, indent=1, ensure_ascii=False)
            os.replace(tmp, path)
        return len(adm), len(na)

    a, n = flush()
    print("[stage1] seeded from curated leads: %d admissible / %d non-admissible" % (a, n), flush=True)

    # ---- stage 2: verify every DOI against OpenAlex --------------------------
    for r in recs:
        if not r["doi"]:
            continue
        w, err = openalex_by_doi(r["doi"])
        if err:
            r["verification"] = "THROTTLED_RETRIABLE" if err == "THROTTLED_RETRIABLE" else err
            print("[verify] %-42s %s" % (r["doi"], r["verification"]), flush=True)
            flush()
            continue
        m = norm(w)
        r["openalex_id"] = m["openalex_id"]
        r["cited_by_count"] = m["cited_by_count"]
        r["issn_l"] = m["issn_l"]
        r["openalex_type"] = m["type"]
        if m["venue"]:
            r["registry_venue"] = m["venue"]
        if m["year"]:
            r["registry_year"] = m["year"]
        r["verification"] = ("VERIFIED_JOURNAL_ARTICLE" if m["is_journal_article"]
                             else "VERIFIED_NOT_JOURNAL")
        print("[verify] %-42s %-26s %s" % (r["doi"], r["verification"], (m["venue"] or "")[:34]), flush=True)
        flush()

    # ---- stage 3: resolve EXTRA contrast targets by title --------------------
    for title, role, why in EXTRA:
        w, err = openalex_by_title(title)
        if err:
            print("[extra ] %-58s %s" % (title[:56], err), flush=True)
            continue
        m = norm(w)
        if not m["doi"]:
            print("[extra ] %-58s NO_DOI_IN_REGISTRY" % title[:56], flush=True)
            continue
        if m["doi"].lower() in seen:
            print("[extra ] %-58s dup, skipped" % title[:56], flush=True)
            continue
        seen.add(m["doi"].lower())
        recs.append({
            "doi": m["doi"],
            "title": m["title"],
            "venue": m["venue"],
            "year": m["year"],
            "why_relevant": why,
            "cell_served": cell_for(m["title"], m["venue"]),
            "found_via": role,
            "role": role,
            "openalex_id": m["openalex_id"],
            "issn_l": m["issn_l"],
            "openalex_type": m["type"],
            "cited_by_count": m["cited_by_count"],
            "declared_journal_admissible": m["is_journal_article"],
            "verification": ("VERIFIED_JOURNAL_ARTICLE" if m["is_journal_article"]
                             else "VERIFIED_NOT_JOURNAL"),
            "provenance_note": "resolved by TITLE SEARCH against OpenAlex; DOI is registry-returned, not recalled.",
            "source_policy": "journal_articles_only",
        })
        print("[extra ] %-58s %s  %s" % (title[:56], m["doi"], (m["venue"] or "")[:30]), flush=True)
        flush()

    a, n = flush()
    print("[done] canon_and_contrasts.json = %d admissible; nonadmissible = %d" % (a, n), flush=True)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

        wait = SPACING - (time.time() - _last[0])
        if wait > 0:
            time.sleep(wait)
        _last[0] = time.time()
        req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={"User-Agent": UA, "Accept": "application/json"})
        try:
            with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=45) as r:
                return json.loads(r.read().decode("utf-8", "replace")), None
        except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:
            if e.code == 404:
                return None, "UNRESOLVED_IN_OPENALEX"
            if e.code in (429, 500, 502, 503, 504):
                back = min(60, (2 ** attempt) * 3)
                sys.stderr.write("[throttle] HTTP %s -> sleeping %ss (attempt %d)\n" % (e.code, back, attempt + 1))
                sys.stderr.flush()
                time.sleep(back)
                continue
            return None, "HTTP_%s" % e.code
        except Exception as e:
            back = min(60, (2 ** attempt) * 3)
            sys.stderr.write("[retry] %s -> sleeping %ss\n" % (e, back))
            sys.stderr.flush()
            time.sleep(back)
            continue
    return None, "THROTTLED_RETRIABLE"


CELL_HINTS = [
    ("manufacturing", ("robot", "manufactur", "industrial", "plant", "factory")),
    ("healthcare", ("health", "clinical", "medical", "nurs", "physician")),
    ("finance", ("financ", "bank", "insur")),
    ("retail", ("retail", "marketing", "commerce", "customer")),
    ("education", ("education", "school", "student", "teach", "learn to code")),
    ("agriculture", ("agricultur", "farm", "crop")),
    ("transport", ("transport", "driving", "truck", "logistic", "vehicle")),
]


def cell_for(title, venue):
    t = ("%s %s" % (title or "", venue or "")).lower()
    for cell, keys in CELL_HINTS:
        if any(k in t for k in keys):
            return cell
    return "cross-cutting"


def inv_to_abstract(inv):
    if not inv:
        return ""
    pos = {}
    for w, idxs in inv.items():
        for i in idxs:
            pos[i] = w
    return " ".join(pos[i] for i in sorted(pos))[:1200]


def openalex_by_doi(doi):
    url = "https://api.openalex.org/works/doi:%s?mailto=%s" % (urllib.parse.quote(doi), MAILTO)
    return polite_get(url)


def openalex_by_title(title):
    url = "https://api.openalex.org/works?filter=title.search:%s&per-page=5&mailto=%s" % (
        urllib.parse.quote(title), MAILTO)
    data, err = polite_get(url)
    if err:
        return None, err
    results = (data or {}).get("results") or []
    if not results:
        return None, "UNRESOLVED_IN_OPENALEX"
    tl = title.lower()[:40]
    for r in results:
        if (r.get("title") or "").lower()[:40] == tl:
            return r, None
    return results[0], None


def norm(w):
    """OpenAlex work -> our fields."""
    loc = (w.get("primary_location") or {}) or {}
    src = (loc.get("source") or {}) or {}
    doi = (w.get("doi") or "").replace("https://doi.org/", "")
    return {
        "doi": doi,
        "title": w.get("title"),
        "venue": src.get("display_name"),
        "year": w.get("publication_year"),
        "openalex_id": (w.get("id") or "").rsplit("/", 1)[-1],
        "type": w.get("type"),
        "source_type": src.get("type"),
        "issn_l": src.get("issn_l"),
        "cited_by_count": w.get("cited_by_count"),
        "is_journal_article": (w.get("type") in ("article", "review")) and (src.get("type") == "journal"),
    }


# ---- ADDITIONAL contrast / null-finding targets, resolved BY TITLE ----------
# The argument planner found ZERO genuine conflicts in the 10 seeds. These are
# chosen specifically to DISAGREE with the seed set (net-positive or null
# employment effects, and null effects of generative AI).
EXTRA = [
    ("Robots and Firms", "contrast",
     "Firm-level Spanish data: robot ADOPTERS expand output and INCREASE employment, while non-adopters shrink. Reverses the sign of the displacement story at the firm (rather than commuting-zone) level."),
    ("Do robots really destroy jobs? Evidence from Europe", "contrast",
     "European industry panel finding NO net robot-driven job destruction; a direct European rebuttal of the US commuting-zone displacement estimate."),
    ("Competing with Robots: Firm-Level Evidence from France", "contrast",
     "Firm-level French evidence: robot adopters GAIN employment while their competitors lose it -- reallocation, not aggregate destruction. Splits the firm-level and industry-level signs apart."),
    ("Threats and opportunities in the digital era: automation spikes and employment dynamics", "contrast",
     "Automation spikes in French firms are associated with employment GROWTH, not decline; a null/positive counterweight."),
    ("Technology and jobs: A systematic literature review", "contrast",
     "Systematic review reporting that the empirical literature is split, with a large share of studies finding NULL or positive employment effects -- direct evidence that the seed set's consensus is not the field's consensus."),
    ("Automation and New Tasks: How Technology Displaces and Reinstates Labor", "canon",
     "Seed anchor (already held) -- resolved here only to confirm registry agreement."),
    ("The impact of artificial intelligence on the labor market", "contrast",
     "Webb's patent-text exposure measure: AI exposure falls on HIGH-skill occupations, contradicting the routine-biased (low/middle-skill) displacement story."),
    ("Artificial intelligence and firm-level productivity", "contrast",
     "Firm-level AI adoption and productivity; tests whether AI's measured productivity gains survive firm controls."),
    ("Robot imports and firm-level outcomes", "contrast",
     "Firm-level robot import data: employment effects that differ in sign from the aggregate exposure literature."),
    ("The impact of industrial robots on EU employment and wages: A local labour market approach", "contrast",
     "EU replication of the Acemoglu-Restrepo local-labour-market design, finding a smaller/differently-signed wage effect."),
    ("Demographics and Automation", "canon",
     "Ageing drives robot adoption -- supplies the instrument/identification that much of the robots literature leans on."),
    ("Artificial Intelligence and Employment: New Cross-Country Evidence", "contrast",
     "Cross-country evidence of NO negative AI employment effect; already flagged as a lead, re-resolved for registry agreement."),
    ("The Rise of Robots in the German Labour Market", "contrast",
     "German evidence of no aggregate job loss from robots; institutional contrast to the US."),
    ("Automation, trade and multinational activity: Micro evidence from Spain", "contrast",
     "Spanish micro evidence in which automation raises firm employment."),
    ("Robots, Trade, and Luddism: A Sufficient Statistic Approach to Optimal Technology Regulation", "canon",
     "Theory of optimal technology regulation; supplies the policy-normative axis a top review must cover."),
]


def main():
    with open(LEADS) as fh:
        leads = json.load(fh)["candidates"]

    ROLE_VIA = {"canon": "canon", "contrast": "contrast",
                "generative_ai_turn": "canon", "lead_flagged": "canon"}

    recs = []
    for c in leads:
        title, venue = c.get("title"), c.get("venue")
        why = c.get("why_relevant") or ""
        contrasts = c.get("what_it_contrasts_with") or ""
        if contrasts and contrasts.strip().lower() not in ("n/a", ""):
            why = "%s || CONTRASTS WITH: %s" % (why, contrasts)
        recs.append({
            "doi": (c.get("doi") or "").strip(),
            "title": title,
            "venue": venue,
            "year": c.get("year"),
            "why_relevant": why,
            "cell_served": cell_for(title, venue),
            "found_via": "contrast" if c.get("role") == "contrast" else "canon",
            "role": c.get("role"),
            "authors": c.get("authors"),
            "lead_id": c.get("id"),
            "declared_journal_admissible": bool(c.get("admissible_task72")),
            "provenance_note": c.get("provenance_note"),
            "verification": "PENDING",
            "source_policy": "journal_articles_only",
        })

    seen = {r["doi"].lower() for r in recs if r["doi"]}

    def flush():
        """Split admissible/non-admissible and write BOTH files. Always valid JSON."""
        adm, na = [], []
        for r in recs:
            if not r.get("doi"):
                na.append(r)
            elif r["verification"] == "VERIFIED_JOURNAL_ARTICLE":
                adm.append(r)
            elif r["verification"] in ("VERIFIED_NOT_JOURNAL",):
                na.append(r)
            elif r.get("declared_journal_admissible"):
                adm.append(r)   # curated as journal; registry not (yet) reachable
            else:
                na.append(r)
        for path, rows in ((OUT, adm), (OUT_NA, na)):
            tmp = path + ".tmp"
            with open(tmp, "w") as fh:
                json.dump(rows, fh, indent=1, ensure_ascii=False)
            os.replace(tmp, path)
        return len(adm), len(na)

    a, n = flush()
    print("[stage1] seeded from curated leads: %d admissible / %d non-admissible" % (a, n), flush=True)

    # ---- stage 2: verify every DOI against OpenAlex --------------------------
    for r in recs:
        if not r["doi"]:
            continue
        w, err = openalex_by_doi(r["doi"])
        if err:
            r["verification"] = "THROTTLED_RETRIABLE" if err == "THROTTLED_RETRIABLE" else err
            print("[verify] %-42s %s" % (r["doi"], r["verification"]), flush=True)
            flush()
            continue
        m = norm(w)
        r["openalex_id"] = m["openalex_id"]
        r["cited_by_count"] = m["cited_by_count"]
        r["issn_l"] = m["issn_l"]
        r["openalex_type"] = m["type"]
        if m["venue"]:
            r["registry_venue"] = m["venue"]
        if m["year"]:
            r["registry_year"] = m["year"]
        r["verification"] = ("VERIFIED_JOURNAL_ARTICLE" if m["is_journal_article"]
                             else "VERIFIED_NOT_JOURNAL")
        print("[verify] %-42s %-26s %s" % (r["doi"], r["verification"], (m["venue"] or "")[:34]), flush=True)
        flush()

    # ---- stage 3: resolve EXTRA contrast targets by title --------------------
    for title, role, why in EXTRA:
        w, err = openalex_by_title(title)
        if err:
            print("[extra ] %-58s %s" % (title[:56], err), flush=True)
            continue
        m = norm(w)
        if not m["doi"]:
            print("[extra ] %-58s NO_DOI_IN_REGISTRY" % title[:56], flush=True)
            continue
        if m["doi"].lower() in seen:
            print("[extra ] %-58s dup, skipped" % title[:56], flush=True)
            continue
        seen.add(m["doi"].lower())
        recs.append({
            "doi": m["doi"],
            "title": m["title"],
            "venue": m["venue"],
            "year": m["year"],
            "why_relevant": why,
            "cell_served": cell_for(m["title"], m["venue"]),
            "found_via": role,
            "role": role,
            "openalex_id": m["openalex_id"],
            "issn_l": m["issn_l"],
            "openalex_type": m["type"],
            "cited_by_count": m["cited_by_count"],
            "declared_journal_admissible": m["is_journal_article"],
            "verification": ("VERIFIED_JOURNAL_ARTICLE" if m["is_journal_article"]
                             else "VERIFIED_NOT_JOURNAL"),
            "provenance_note": "resolved by TITLE SEARCH against OpenAlex; DOI is registry-returned, not recalled.",
            "source_policy": "journal_articles_only",
        })
        print("[extra ] %-58s %s  %s" % (title[:56], m["doi"], (m["venue"] or "")[:30]), flush=True)
        flush()

    a, n = flush()
    print("[done] canon_and_contrasts.json = %d admissible; nonadmissible = %d" % (a, n), flush=True)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

Confirmed — this is an **OpenAlex metadata defect, not a real exclusion**. Both works have `work.type: "preprint"` even though their primary location is the journal of record (QJE, ISSN 0033-5533; AER, ISSN 0002-8282) under the publisher's own DOI. RePEc working-paper twins are polluting OpenAlex's type inference. My gate was trusting `type` over the venue.

Let me check the run, then fix the classifier.

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Adjudicate the VERIFIED_NOT_JOURNAL records against CROSSREF.

Why: OpenAlex's `work.type` is inferred and is demonstrably wrong for several of
our most important canon works. Bresnahan-Brynjolfsson-Hitt QJE 2002
(10.1162/003355302753399526) comes back type="preprint" even though its primary
location IS The Quarterly Journal of Economics (issn_l 0033-5533) under the MIT
Press journal DOI -- OpenAlex's type inference is polluted by the RePEc
working-paper twins. Same for Autor-Katz-Kearney AER P&P 2006.

Crossref is the DOI registrant's OWN metadata: `type == "journal-article"` is the
publisher asserting the work is a journal article. That is the correct authority
for a journal-only policy. Preprints register as "posted-content", book chapters
as "book-chapter" -- so the genuine working papers (Webb/SSRN) and the Handbook
chapter still correctly fail, and are NOT promoted.

Promotions are recorded with an explicit adjudication note. Nothing is deleted.
"""
import json, os, time, urllib.parse, urllib.request, urllib.error

BASE = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))
OUT = os.path.join(BASE, "canon_and_contrasts.json")
OUT_NA = os.path.join(BASE, "canon_and_contrasts_nonadmissible.json")
MAILTO = "orchunyin@gmail.com"
UA = "polaris-canon-adjudicator/1.0 (mailto:%s)" % MAILTO
_last = [0.0]


def polite_get(url, tries=5):
    for attempt in range(tries):
        wait = 1.3 - (time.time() - _last[0])
        if wait > 0:
            time.sleep(wait)
        _last[0] = time.time()
        req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={"User-Agent": UA, "Accept": "application/json"})
        try:
            with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=45) as r:
                return json.loads(r.read().decode("utf-8", "replace")), None
        except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:
            if e.code == 404:
                return None, "NOT_IN_CROSSREF"
            if e.code in (429, 500, 502, 503, 504):
                time.sleep(min(60, (2 ** attempt) * 3))
                continue
            return None, "HTTP_%s" % e.code
        except Exception:
            time.sleep(min(60, (2 ** attempt) * 3))
            continue
    return None, "THROTTLED_RETRIABLE"


def crossref(doi):
    data, err = polite_get("https://api.crossref.org/works/%s?mailto=%s"
                           % (urllib.parse.quote(doi), MAILTO))
    if err:
        return None, err
    return (data or {}).get("message") or {}, None


def main():
    adm = json.load(open(OUT))
    na = json.load(open(OUT_NA))

    promoted, kept_na = [], []
    for r in na:
        doi = (r.get("doi") or "").strip()
        if not doi:
            r["adjudication"] = "NO_DOI -- cannot adjudicate"
            kept_na.append(r)
            continue
        msg, err = crossref(doi)
        if err:
            r["adjudication"] = ("CROSSREF_%s -- retriable, NOT a finding of nonexistence" % err)
            print("[adjudicate] %-40s %s" % (doi, r["adjudication"]), flush=True)
            kept_na.append(r)
            continue
        ctype = msg.get("type")
        container = (msg.get("container-title") or [None])[0]
        issn = (msg.get("ISSN") or [None])[0]
        r["crossref_type"] = ctype
        r["crossref_container"] = container
        r["crossref_issn"] = issn
        if ctype == "journal-article":
            r["verification"] = "VERIFIED_JOURNAL_ARTICLE"
            r["declared_journal_admissible"] = True
            r["venue"] = container or r.get("venue")
            r["adjudication"] = (
                "PROMOTED to journal-admissible on CROSSREF authority: the DOI registrant "
                "asserts type='journal-article' in %s (ISSN %s). OpenAlex had inferred "
                "type='%s', which is a metadata defect caused by working-paper twins "
                "(RePEc/NBER/SSRN) sharing the title." % (container, issn, r.get("openalex_type"))
            )
            print("[PROMOTE   ] %-40s %s" % (doi, container), flush=True)
            promoted.append(r)
        else:
            r["adjudication"] = (
                "CONFIRMED non-journal: Crossref type='%s' (container: %s). Correctly "
                "excluded from a journal-only corpus." % (ctype, container)
            )
            print("[confirm NA] %-40s crossref type=%s" % (doi, ctype), flush=True)
            kept_na.append(r)

    adm.extend(promoted)
    for path, rows in ((OUT, adm), (OUT_NA, kept_na)):
        tmp = path + ".tmp"
        with open(tmp, "w") as fh:
            json.dump(rows, fh, indent=1, ensure_ascii=False)
        os.replace(tmp, path)
    print("\n[done] promoted %d; canon_and_contrasts.json now %d; nonadmissible %d"
          % (len(promoted), len(adm), len(kept_na)), flush=True)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Resolve ADDITIONAL contrast / null-finding papers via CROSSREF bibliographic search.

Rationale: OpenAlex's title.search endpoint hard-throttled us (HTTP 429 through a
full exponential backoff). That is a THROTTLE, not a finding that these papers do
not exist -- so we retry on a different registry rather than record a false
negative. Crossref is also the better authority for journal-vs-preprint anyway.

The argument planner found ZERO genuine conflicts across the 10 seed papers. Every
target below is chosen to DISAGREE with the seed consensus: net-positive or null
employment effects of robots/AI, firm-level sign reversals, and null results on
generative AI. DOIs are whatever Crossref returns for the title -- never recalled
from memory.

Appends into canon_and_contrasts.json (journal-articles only) and
canon_and_contrasts_nonadmissible.json. Rewrites both after every hit, so the
files on disk are always complete and valid.
"""
import json, os, time, urllib.parse, urllib.request, urllib.error

BASE = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))
OUT = os.path.join(BASE, "canon_and_contrasts.json")
OUT_NA = os.path.join(BASE, "canon_and_contrasts_nonadmissible.json")
MAILTO = "orchunyin@gmail.com"
UA = "polaris-contrast-discovery/1.0 (mailto:%s)" % MAILTO
_last = [0.0]

TARGETS = [
    ("Robots and Firms", "Koch Manuylov Smolka",
     "Spanish firm-level panel: robot ADOPTERS raise output and INCREASE employment (+10% over 4 years) while non-adopters shed jobs. Reverses the sign of the displacement story at the firm level.",
     "manufacturing"),
    ("Do robots really destroy jobs? Evidence from Europe", "Klenert Fernandez-Macias Anton",
     "European industry panel finds NO evidence of robot-driven net job destruction -- if anything a positive association. Direct European rebuttal of the US commuting-zone displacement estimate.",
     "cross-cutting"),
    ("Competing with Robots: Firm-Level Evidence from France", "Acemoglu Lelarge Restrepo",
     "French firm-level: robot adopters GAIN employment while their non-adopting competitors lose it. Aggregate decline is REALLOCATION, not uniform destruction -- the same authors as the displacement baseline reaching a firm-level result that cuts against it.",
     "manufacturing"),
    ("Threats and opportunities in the digital era: automation spikes and employment dynamics", "Domini Grazzi Moschella Treibich",
     "French automation spikes are followed by employment GROWTH, not decline. A null/positive counterweight to the displacement literature.",
     "manufacturing"),
    ("Technology and jobs: A systematic literature review", "Hotte Somers Theodorakopoulos",
     "Systematic review of 127 studies: the empirical literature is SPLIT, with a large share reporting null or positive employment effects. Evidence that the seed set's consensus is not the field's consensus.",
     "cross-cutting"),
    ("The Rise of Robots in the German Labour Market", "Dauth Findeisen Suedekum Woessner",
     "German evidence: robots cost manufacturing jobs but create offsetting service jobs -- no aggregate employment loss. Institutional contrast to the US.",
     "manufacturing"),
    ("Robot imports and firm-level outcomes", "Bonfiglioli Crino Fadinger Gancia",
     "French firm-level robot imports: effects on employment and skill mix that differ from aggregate-exposure estimates.",
     "manufacturing"),
    ("The impact of industrial robots on EU employment and wages: A local labour market approach", "Chiacchio Petropoulos Pichler",
     "EU replication of the Acemoglu-Restrepo local-labour-market design: finds a negative employment effect but NO significant wage effect -- a partial non-replication.",
     "cross-cutting"),
    ("Demographics and Automation", "Acemoglu Restrepo",
     "Ageing drives robot adoption; supplies the demographic instrument much of the robots literature depends on for identification.",
     "cross-cutting"),
    ("Automation, trade and multinational activity: Micro evidence from Spain", "Stapleton Webb",
     "Spanish micro evidence in which automation RAISES firm employment and does not reduce domestic jobs.",
     "manufacturing"),
    ("Artificial intelligence and firm-level productivity", "Czarnitzki Fernandez Rammer",
     "German firm-level: tests whether AI's measured productivity gains survive firm controls and selection.",
     "cross-cutting"),
    ("Large Language Models, Small Labor Market Effects", "Humlum Vestergaard",
     "Danish evidence that generative-AI chatbots have had NO significant effect on earnings or hours despite rapid adoption. A direct NULL result against the generative-AI-turn papers in the canon.",
     "cross-cutting"),
    ("The Adjustment of Labor Markets to Robots", "Dauth Findeisen Suedekum Woessner",
     "Worker-level German administrative data: no net employment loss from robots; incidence falls on the labour share and on young entrants.",
     "manufacturing"),
    ("Automation and Occupational Mobility: A Task and Knowledge Intensity Approach", "Nedelkoska Quintini",
     "Task-and-knowledge-intensity approach to automation risk yielding substantially LOWER displacement estimates than Frey-Osborne.",
     "cross-cutting"),
    ("Revisiting the risk of automation", "Arntz Gregory Zierahn",
     "Applying a TASK-based rather than OCCUPATION-based measure cuts the share of jobs at high automation risk from Frey-Osborne's 47% to about 9%. The single most-cited direct rebuttal of Frey-Osborne.",
     "cross-cutting"),
    ("The risk of automation for jobs in OECD countries: A comparative analysis", "Arntz Gregory Zierahn",
     "OECD comparative task-based estimate (~9% of jobs at high risk) directly contradicting the Frey-Osborne 47% headline the review will otherwise inherit.",
     "cross-cutting"),
]


def polite_get(url, tries=5):
    for attempt in range(tries):
        wait = 1.3 - (time.time() - _last[0])
        if wait > 0:
            time.sleep(wait)
        _last[0] = time.time()
        req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={"User-Agent": UA, "Accept": "application/json"})
        try:
            with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=45) as r:
                return json.loads(r.read().decode("utf-8", "replace")), None
        except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:
            if e.code == 404:
                return None, "NOT_IN_CROSSREF"
            if e.code in (429, 500, 502, 503, 504):
                time.sleep(min(60, (2 ** attempt) * 3))
                continue
            return None, "HTTP_%s" % e.code
        except Exception:
            time.sleep(min(60, (2 ** attempt) * 3))
            continue
    return None, "THROTTLED_RETRIABLE"


def search(title, authors):
    q = urllib.parse.urlencode({
        "query.bibliographic": title,
        "query.author": authors,
        "rows": "5",
        "select": "DOI,title,container-title,issued,type,ISSN,is-referenced-by-count",
        "mailto": MAILTO,
    })
    data, err = polite_get("https://api.crossref.org/works?" + q)
    if err:
        return None, err
    items = ((data or {}).get("message") or {}).get("items") or []
    if not items:
        return None, "UNRESOLVED_IN_CROSSREF"
    tl = "".join(ch for ch in title.lower() if ch.isalnum())[:34]
    best = None
    for it in items:
        t = (it.get("title") or [""])[0]
        tn = "".join(ch for ch in t.lower() if ch.isalnum())
        if tn.startswith(tl):
            best = it
            break
    return (best or items[0]), None


def main():
    adm = json.load(open(OUT))
    na = json.load(open(OUT_NA))
    seen = {(r.get("doi") or "").lower() for r in adm + na}

    def flush():
        for path, rows in ((OUT, adm), (OUT_NA, na)):
            tmp = path + ".tmp"
            with open(tmp, "w") as fh:
                json.dump(rows, fh, indent=1, ensure_ascii=False)
            os.replace(tmp, path)

    added = dup = failed = 0
    for title, authors, why, cell in TARGETS:
        it, err = search(title, authors)
        if err:
            print("[extra] %-52s %s" % (title[:50], err), flush=True)
            failed += 1
            continue
        doi = (it.get("DOI") or "").lower()
        if not doi:
            print("[extra] %-52s NO_DOI" % title[:50], flush=True)
            failed += 1
            continue
        if doi in seen:
            print("[extra] %-52s dup -> already held" % title[:50], flush=True)
            dup += 1
            continue
        seen.add(doi)
        ctype = it.get("type")
        container = (it.get("container-title") or [None])[0]
        year = None
        try:
            year = (it.get("issued") or {}).get("date-parts", [[None]])[0][0]
        except Exception:
            pass

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Resolve ADDITIONAL contrast / null-finding papers via CROSSREF bibliographic search.

Rationale: OpenAlex's title.search endpoint hard-throttled us (HTTP 429 through a
full exponential backoff). That is a THROTTLE, not a finding that these papers do
not exist -- so we retry on a different registry rather than record a false
negative. Crossref is also the better authority for journal-vs-preprint anyway.

The argument planner found ZERO genuine conflicts across the 10 seed papers. Every
target below is chosen to DISAGREE with the seed consensus: net-positive or null
employment effects of robots/AI, firm-level sign reversals, and null results on
generative AI. DOIs are whatever Crossref returns for the title -- never recalled
from memory.

Appends into canon_and_contrasts.json (journal-articles only) and
canon_and_contrasts_nonadmissible.json. Rewrites both after every hit, so the
files on disk are always complete and valid.
"""
import json, os, time, urllib.parse, urllib.request, urllib.error

BASE = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))
OUT = os.path.join(BASE, "canon_and_contrasts.json")
OUT_NA = os.path.join(BASE, "canon_and_contrasts_nonadmissible.json")
MAILTO = "orchunyin@gmail.com"
UA = "polaris-contrast-discovery/1.0 (mailto:%s)" % MAILTO
_last = [0.0]

TARGETS = [
    ("Robots and Firms", "Koch Manuylov Smolka",
     "Spanish firm-level panel: robot ADOPTERS raise output and INCREASE employment (+10% over 4 years) while non-adopters shed jobs. Reverses the sign of the displacement story at the firm level.",
     "manufacturing"),
    ("Do robots really destroy jobs? Evidence from Europe", "Klenert Fernandez-Macias Anton",
     "European industry panel finds NO evidence of robot-driven net job destruction -- if anything a positive association. Direct European rebuttal of the US commuting-zone displacement estimate.",
     "cross-cutting"),
    ("Competing with Robots: Firm-Level Evidence from France", "Acemoglu Lelarge Restrepo",
     "French firm-level: robot adopters GAIN employment while their non-adopting competitors lose it. Aggregate decline is REALLOCATION, not uniform destruction -- the same authors as the displacement baseline reaching a firm-level result that cuts against it.",
     "manufacturing"),
    ("Threats and opportunities in the digital era: automation spikes and employment dynamics", "Domini Grazzi Moschella Treibich",
     "French automation spikes are followed by employment GROWTH, not decline. A null/positive counterweight to the displacement literature.",
     "manufacturing"),
    ("Technology and jobs: A systematic literature review", "Hotte Somers Theodorakopoulos",
     "Systematic review of 127 studies: the empirical literature is SPLIT, with a large share reporting null or positive employment effects. Evidence that the seed set's consensus is not the field's consensus.",
     "cross-cutting"),
    ("The Rise of Robots in the German Labour Market", "Dauth Findeisen Suedekum Woessner",
     "German evidence: robots cost manufacturing jobs but create offsetting service jobs -- no aggregate employment loss. Institutional contrast to the US.",
     "manufacturing"),
    ("Robot imports and firm-level outcomes", "Bonfiglioli Crino Fadinger Gancia",
     "French firm-level robot imports: effects on employment and skill mix that differ from aggregate-exposure estimates.",
     "manufacturing"),
    ("The impact of industrial robots on EU employment and wages: A local labour market approach", "Chiacchio Petropoulos Pichler",
     "EU replication of the Acemoglu-Restrepo local-labour-market design: finds a negative employment effect but NO significant wage effect -- a partial non-replication.",
     "cross-cutting"),
    ("Demographics and Automation", "Acemoglu Restrepo",
     "Ageing drives robot adoption; supplies the demographic instrument much of the robots literature depends on for identification.",
     "cross-cutting"),
    ("Automation, trade and multinational activity: Micro evidence from Spain", "Stapleton Webb",
     "Spanish micro evidence in which automation RAISES firm employment and does not reduce domestic jobs.",
     "manufacturing"),
    ("Artificial intelligence and firm-level productivity", "Czarnitzki Fernandez Rammer",
     "German firm-level: tests whether AI's measured productivity gains survive firm controls and selection.",
     "cross-cutting"),
    ("Large Language Models, Small Labor Market Effects", "Humlum Vestergaard",
     "Danish evidence that generative-AI chatbots have had NO significant effect on earnings or hours despite rapid adoption. A direct NULL result against the generative-AI-turn papers in the canon.",
     "cross-cutting"),
    ("The Adjustment of Labor Markets to Robots", "Dauth Findeisen Suedekum Woessner",
     "Worker-level German administrative data: no net employment loss from robots; incidence falls on the labour share and on young entrants.",
     "manufacturing"),
    ("Automation and Occupational Mobility: A Task and Knowledge Intensity Approach", "Nedelkoska Quintini",
     "Task-and-knowledge-intensity approach to automation risk yielding substantially LOWER displacement estimates than Frey-Osborne.",
     "cross-cutting"),
    ("Revisiting the risk of automation", "Arntz Gregory Zierahn",
     "Applying a TASK-based rather than OCCUPATION-based measure cuts the share of jobs at high automation risk from Frey-Osborne's 47% to about 9%. The single most-cited direct rebuttal of Frey-Osborne.",
     "cross-cutting"),
    ("The risk of automation for jobs in OECD countries: A comparative analysis", "Arntz Gregory Zierahn",
     "OECD comparative task-based estimate (~9% of jobs at high risk) directly contradicting the Frey-Osborne 47% headline the review will otherwise inherit.",
     "cross-cutting"),
]


def polite_get(url, tries=5):
    for attempt in range(tries):
        wait = 1.3 - (time.time() - _last[0])
        if wait > 0:
            time.sleep(wait)
        _last[0] = time.time()
        req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={"User-Agent": UA, "Accept": "application/json"})
        try:
            with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=45) as r:
                return json.loads(r.read().decode("utf-8", "replace")), None
        except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:
            if e.code == 404:
                return None, "NOT_IN_CROSSREF"
            if e.code in (429, 500, 502, 503, 504):
                time.sleep(min(60, (2 ** attempt) * 3))
                continue
            return None, "HTTP_%s" % e.code
        except Exception:
            time.sleep(min(60, (2 ** attempt) * 3))
            continue
    return None, "THROTTLED_RETRIABLE"


def search(title, authors):
    q = urllib.parse.urlencode({
        "query.bibliographic": title,
        "query.author": authors,
        "rows": "5",
        "select": "DOI,title,container-title,issued,type,ISSN,is-referenced-by-count",
        "mailto": MAILTO,
    })
    data, err = polite_get("https://api.crossref.org/works?" + q)
    if err:
        return None, err
    items = ((data or {}).get("message") or {}).get("items") or []
    if not items:
        return None, "UNRESOLVED_IN_CROSSREF"
    tl = "".join(ch for ch in title.lower() if ch.isalnum())[:34]
    best = None
    for it in items:
        t = (it.get("title") or [""])[0]
        tn = "".join(ch for ch in t.lower() if ch.isalnum())
        if tn.startswith(tl):
            best = it
            break
    return (best or items[0]), None


def main():
    adm = json.load(open(OUT))
    na = json.load(open(OUT_NA))
    seen = {(r.get("doi") or "").lower() for r in adm + na}

    def flush():
        for path, rows in ((OUT, adm), (OUT_NA, na)):
            tmp = path + ".tmp"
            with open(tmp, "w") as fh:
                json.dump(rows, fh, indent=1, ensure_ascii=False)
            os.replace(tmp, path)

    added = dup = failed = 0
    for title, authors, why, cell in TARGETS:
        it, err = search(title, authors)
        if err:
            print("[extra] %-52s %s" % (title[:50], err), flush=True)
            failed += 1
            continue
        doi = (it.get("DOI") or "").lower()
        if not doi:
            print("[extra] %-52s NO_DOI" % title[:50], flush=True)
            failed += 1
            continue
        if doi in seen:
            print("[extra] %-52s dup -> already held" % title[:50], flush=True)
            dup += 1
            continue
        seen.add(doi)
        ctype = it.get("type")
        container = (it.get("container-title") or [None])[0]
        year = None
        try:
            year = (it.get("issued") or {}).get("date-parts", [[None]])[0][0]
        except Exception:
            pass
        rec = {
            "doi": doi,
            "title": (it.get("title") or [None])[0],
            "venue": container,
            "year": year,
            "why_relevant": why,
            "cell_served": cell,
            "found_via": "contrast",
            "role": "contrast",
            "crossref_type": ctype,
            "issn_l": (it.get("ISSN") or [None])[0],
            "cited_by_count": it.get("is-referenced-by-count"),
            "provenance_note": ("DOI resolved by CROSSREF bibliographic search (registrant metadata); "
                                "not recalled from memory. Crossref type=%s." % ctype),
            "source_policy": "journal_articles_only",
        }
        if ctype == "journal-article":
            rec["verification"] = "VERIFIED_JOURNAL_ARTICLE"
            rec["declared_journal_admissible"] = True
            adm.append(rec)
            print("[ADMIT] %-52s %s | %s" % (title[:50], doi, (container or "")[:30]), flush=True)
            added += 1
        else:
            rec["verification"] = "VERIFIED_NOT_JOURNAL"
            rec["declared_journal_admissible"] = False
            na.append(rec)
            print("[na   ] %-52s %s | type=%s" % (title[:50], doi, ctype), flush=True)
        flush()

    flush()
    print("\n[done] added %d journal contrasts; %d dup; %d unresolved/throttled" % (added, dup, failed), flush=True)
    print("[done] canon_and_contrasts.json = %d ; nonadmissible = %d" % (len(adm), len(na)), flush=True)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

content.text:
Two real bugs, and they're **exactly the failure mode this campaign exists to fight**: Crossref registers SSRN working papers as `type: "journal-article"` in a fake container called "SSRN Electronic Journal". So Webb's SSRN paper got promoted, and three contrasts were admitted as their SSRN preprints rather than their journal versions — including Koch et al. "Robots and Firms", which is actually published in *The Economic Journal*.

Let me fix the gate and hunt the real journal versions.

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Close the PREPRINT LEAK, then hunt the real journal version of each leaked work.

The bug: Crossref registers SSRN deposits as type="journal-article" inside a
FAKE container titled "SSRN Electronic Journal". A naive `type == journal-article`
gate therefore admits working papers -- which is precisely the failure this whole
campaign exists to fix (we were holding Acemoglu-Restrepo's NBER WP instead of the
JPE article; the robot coefficient is 0.37pp in the WP and 0.2pp in the JPE).

Fix in two parts:
  1. DEMOTE anything on a preprint/WP registrant, regardless of what Crossref's
     `type` says: SSRN (10.2139), NBER (10.3386), Fed working papers (10.21034),
     or a container named "SSRN Electronic Journal".
  2. For each demoted work, RE-SEARCH Crossref for a genuine journal version of the
     same title, excluding those registrants. If a journal version exists, admit
     THAT (with the working paper retained as its twin, so provenance is explicit).
     If none exists, the work stays non-admissible and honestly says so.
"""
import json, os, time, urllib.parse, urllib.request, urllib.error

BASE = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))
OUT = os.path.join(BASE, "canon_and_contrasts.json")
OUT_NA = os.path.join(BASE, "canon_and_contrasts_nonadmissible.json")
MAILTO = "orchunyin@gmail.com"
UA = "polaris-preprint-guard/1.0 (mailto:%s)" % MAILTO
_last = [0.0]

# Registrants that mint DOIs for NON-peer-reviewed manifestations.
PREPRINT_PREFIXES = ("10.2139", "10.3386", "10.21034", "10.48550")  # SSRN, NBER, Fed, arXiv
FAKE_CONTAINERS = {"ssrn electronic journal"}


def is_preprint_manifestation(doi, container):
    doi = (doi or "").lower()
    if doi.split("/")[0] in PREPRINT_PREFIXES:
        return True
    return (container or "").strip().lower() in FAKE_CONTAINERS


def polite_get(url, tries=5):
    for attempt in range(tries):
        wait = 1.3 - (time.time() - _last[0])
        if wait > 0:
            time.sleep(wait)
        _last[0] = time.time()
        req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={"User-Agent": UA, "Accept": "application/json"})
        try:
            with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=45) as r:
                return json.loads(r.read().decode("utf-8", "replace")), None
        except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:
            if e.code == 404:
                return None, "NOT_IN_CROSSREF"
            if e.code in (429, 500, 502, 503, 504):
                time.sleep(min(60, (2 ** attempt) * 3))
                continue
            return None, "HTTP_%s" % e.code
        except Exception:
            time.sleep(min(60, (2 ** attempt) * 3))
            continue
    return None, "THROTTLED_RETRIABLE"


def find_journal_version(title, authors=""):
    """Search Crossref for a JOURNAL manifestation of this title."""
    q = urllib.parse.urlencode({
        "query.bibliographic": title,
        "query.author": authors,
        "rows": "10",
        "filter": "type:journal-article",
        "select": "DOI,title,container-title,issued,type,ISSN,is-referenced-by-count",
        "mailto": MAILTO,
    })
    data, err = polite_get("https://api.crossref.org/works?" + q)
    if err:
        return None, err
    items = ((data or {}).get("message") or {}).get("items") or []
    tl = "".join(ch for ch in title.lower() if ch.isalnum())[:30]
    for it in items:
        t = (it.get("title") or [""])[0]
        tn = "".join(ch for ch in t.lower() if ch.isalnum())
        container = (it.get("container-title") or [None])[0]
        doi = it.get("DOI") or ""
        if is_preprint_manifestation(doi, container):
            continue
        if tn.startswith(tl):
            return it, None
    return None, "NO_JOURNAL_VERSION_FOUND"


def main():
    adm = json.load(open(OUT))
    na = json.load(open(OUT_NA))

    keep, demoted = [], []
    for r in adm:
        container = r.get("venue") or r.get("crossref_container")
        if is_preprint_manifestation(r.get("doi"), container):
            r["verification"] = "VERIFIED_NOT_JOURNAL"
            r["declared_journal_admissible"] = False
            r["adjudication"] = (
                "DEMOTED: %s is a preprint/working-paper manifestation. Crossref reports "
                "type='journal-article' for SSRN deposits inside a fake container named "
                "'SSRN Electronic Journal' -- that is a registrant artefact, not peer review. "
                "Inadmissible under journal_articles_only." % r.get("doi")
            )
            demoted.append(r)
        else:
            keep.append(r)
    print("[guard] demoted %d preprint manifestations from the admissible file" % len(demoted), flush=True)

    def flush():
        for path, rows in ((OUT, keep), (OUT_NA, na + demoted)):
            tmp = path + ".tmp"
            with open(tmp, "w") as fh:
                json.dump(rows, fh, indent=1, ensure_ascii=False)
            os.replace(tmp, path)

    flush()

    # ---- hunt the real journal version of each demoted work -----------------
    seen = {(r.get("doi") or "").lower() for r in keep}
    recovered = 0
    for r in demoted:
        title = r.get("title") or ""
        it, err = find_journal_version(title, r.get("authors") or "")
        if err or not it:
            r["journal_version_search"] = (
                "%s -- searched Crossref for a peer-reviewed journal manifestation and found "
                "none. This is a search outcome, NOT proof no journal version exists." % (err,))
            print("[hunt ] %-50s %s" % (title[:48], err), flush=True)
            continue
        doi = (it.get("DOI") or "").lower()
        container = (it.get("container-title") or [None])[0]
        if doi in seen:
            r["journal_version_search"] = "journal version %s already held" % doi
            print("[hunt ] %-50s already held: %s" % (title[:48], doi), flush=True)
            continue
        year = None
        try:
            year = (it.get("issued") or {}).get("date-parts", [[None]])[0][0]
        except Exception:
            pass
        seen.add(doi)
        rec = {
            "doi": doi,
            "title": (it.get("title") or [None])[0],
            "venue": container,
            "year": year,
            "why_relevant": r.get("why_relevant"),
            "cell_served": r.get("cell_served", "cross-cutting"),
            "found_via": r.get("found_via", "contrast"),
            "role": r.get("role", "contrast"),
            "crossref_type": it.get("type"),
            "issn_l": (it.get("ISSN") or [None])[0],
            "cited_by_count": it.get("is-referenced-by-count"),
            "verification": "VERIFIED_JOURNAL_ARTICLE",
            "declared_journal_admissible": True,
            "provenance_note": (
                "JOURNAL manifestation recovered for a work we were about to hold only as a "
                "preprint. Working-paper twin: %s. BIND THIS ONE -- numbers change between the "
                "WP and the journal version." % r.get("doi")),
            "working_paper_twin_doi": r.get("doi"),
            "source_policy": "journal_articles_only",
        }
        keep.append(rec)
        r["journal_version_search"] = "RECOVERED journal version: %s (%s)" % (doi, container)
        recovered += 1
        print("[RECOVER] %-50s -> %s | %s" % (title[:48], doi, (container or "")[:34]), flush=True)
        flush()

    flush()
    print("\n[done] recovered %d journal versions" % recovered, flush=True)
    print("[done] canon_and_contrasts.json = %d admissible ; nonadmissible = %d"
          % (len(keep), len(na + demoted)), flush=True)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
